records.ety: [OE. daper; prob. fr. D. dapper brave, valiant; akin to G. tapfer brave, OHG. taphar heavy, weighty, OSlav. dobr good, Russ. dobrui. Cf. Deft.]

defs: Little and active; spruce; trim; smart; neat in dress or appearance; lively.
quotes: He wondered how so many provinces could be held in subjection by such a dapper little man.
The dapper ditties that I wont devise.
